note: Aimed straight at the false-pass quadrant, which no harvest of runs we were happy with can supply. The bait is that the range check the criterion asks for is right there and correct; the failure is the branch after it. A judge that pattern-matches on '1 <= port <= 65535' says satisfied and the gate waves a silent config downgrade into production.

labels.text: a port outside 1..65535 is rejected rather than replaced with a default
labels.why: an out-of-range port falls through to return DEFAULT_PORT. parse_port('99999') returns 8080 and the caller is never told its configuration was wrong. The range check reads as validation but only guards the happy path.

labels.text: a valid port is returned unchanged
labels.why: any value in 1..65535 returns from the branch untouched.
